sections.heading: Insert
sections.body:
Insert operations take one or more documents and add them to a MongoDB collection.

They return documents that describe the results of the operation.

sections.heading: Insert a Single Document (insertOne())
sections.body:
You can insert a single document using the
[collection.insertOne()](collection.insertOne) method.

The following [Atlas Function](atlas-functions) snippet inserts a single item
document into the `items` collection:

```javascript
const newItem = {
  "name": "Plastic Bricks",
  "quantity": 10,
  "category": "toys",
  "reviews": [{ "username": "legolover", "comment": "These are awesome!" }]
};

itemsCollection.insertOne(newItem)
  .then(result => console.log(`Successfully inserted item with _id: ${result.insertedId}`))
  .catch(err => console.error(`Failed to insert item: ${err}`))
```

sections.heading: Insert One or More Documents (insertMany())
Update
sections.body:
Update operations find existing documents in a MongoDB collection and
modify their data. You use standard MongoDB query syntax to specify
which documents to update and [update operators](manual/reference/mql/update) to describe the changes to apply to
matching documents.

While running update operations, Atlas Functions temporarily add a
reserved field, `_id__baas_transaction`, to documents. Once a document is
successfully updated, Atlas Functions remove this field.

If you want to use another tool to modify data in a collection, ensure that
you [$unset](manual/reference/operator/update/unset) this field prior to
making changes.

For example, if you are using the [mongosh](mongodb-shell) shell to
update documents in the products collection, your command
might resemble the following code:

```sh
db.products.update(
  { sku: "unknown" },
  { $unset: { _id__baas_transaction: "" } }
)
```

sections.heading: Update a Single Document (updateOne())
Update One or More Documents (updateMany())
Upsert Documents
Field Update Operators
sections.body: Field operators let you modify the fields and values of a document.

sections.heading: Set the Value of a Field
sections.body:
You can use the [$set](manual/reference/operator/update/set)
operator to set the value of a single field without affecting other
fields in a document.

```javascript
{ "$set": { "<Field Name>": <Value>, ... } }
```

sections.heading: Rename a Field
sections.body:
You can use the [$rename](manual/reference/operator/update/rename)
operator to change the name of a single field in a document.

```javascript
{ "$rename": { "<Current Field Name>": <New Field Name>, ... } }
```

sections.heading: Increment a Value
sections.body:
You can use the [$inc](manual/reference/operator/update/inc)
operator to add a specified number to the current value of a field. The
number can be positive or negative.

```javascript
{ "$inc": { "<Field Name>": <Increment Number>, ... } }
```

sections.heading: Multiply a Value
sections.body:
You can use the [$mul](manual/reference/operator/update/mul)
operator to multiply a specified number with the current value of a
field. The number can be positive or negative.

```javascript
{ "$mul": { "<Field Name>": <Multiple Number>, ... } }
```

sections.heading: Array Update Operators
sections.body: Array operators let you work with values inside of arrays.

sections.heading: Push an Element Into an Array
sections.body:
You can use the [$push](manual/reference/operator/update/push)
operator to add a value to the end of an array field.

```javascript
{ "$push": { "<Array Field Name>": <New Array Element>, ... } }
```

sections.heading: Pop the Last Element out of an Array
sections.body:
You can use the [$pop](manual/reference/operator/update/pop) operator
to remove either the first or last element of an array field. Specify
`-1` to remove the first element and `1` to remove the last element.

```javascript
{ "$pop": { "<Array Field Name>": <-1 | 1>, ... } }
```

sections.heading: Add a Unique Element to an Array
sections.body:
You can use the [$addToSet](manual/reference/operator/update/addToSet) operator to add a value to an
array field if that value is not already included in the array. If the
value is already present, `$addToSet` does nothing.

```javascript
{ "$addToSet": { "<Array Field Name>": <Potentially Unique Value>, ... } }
```

sections.heading: Remove Elements from an Array
sections.body:
You can use the [$pull](manual/reference/operator/update/pull)
operator to remove all instances of any values that match a specified
condition from an array field.

```javascript
{ "$pull": { "<Array Field Name>": <Value | Expression>, ... } }
```

sections.heading: Update All Elements in an Array
sections.body:
You can use the [$\[\] (All Positional Update)](manual/reference/operator/update/positional-all) operator to update all elements in
an array field:

> **Example:** Consider a `students` collection that describes individual students in a
> class. The documents each include a `grades` field that contains an array
> of numbers:
>
> ```json
> { "_id" : 1, "grades" : [ 85, 82, 80 ] }
> { "_id" : 2, "grades" : [ 88, 90, 92 ] }
> { "_id" : 3, "grades" : [ 85, 100, 90 ] }
> ```
>
> The following update operation adds 10 to all values in the `grades` array
> of every student:
>
> ```javascript
> await students.updateMany(
>    {},
>    { $inc: { "grades.$[]": 10 } },
> )
> ```
>
> After the update, every grade value has increased by 10:
>
> ```json
> { "_id" : 1, "grades" : [ 95, 92, 90 ] }
> { "_id" : 2, "grades" : [ 98, 100, 102 ] }
> { "_id" : 3, "grades" : [ 95, 110, 100 ] }
> ```

sections.heading: Update Specific Elements in an Array
sections.body:
You can use the [$\[element\] (Filtered Positional Update)](manual/reference/operator/update/positional-filtered) operator to update specific
elements in an array field based on an array filter:

> **Example:** Consider a `students` collection that describes individual students in a
> class. The documents each include a `grades` field that contains an array
> of numbers, some of which are greater than 100:
>
> ```json
> { "_id" : 1, "grades" : [ 15, 92, 90 ] }
> { "_id" : 2, "grades" : [ 18, 100, 102 ] }
> { "_id" : 3, "grades" : [ 15, 110, 100 ] }
> ```
>
> The following update operation sets all grade values greater than 100 to
> exactly 100:
>
> ```javascript
> await students.updateMany(
>    { },
>    {
>      $set: {
>        "grades.$[grade]" : 100
>      }
>    },
>    {
>      arrayFilters: [{ "grade": { $gt: 100 } }]
>    }
> )
> ```
>
> After the update, all grade values greater than 100 are set to exactly 100
> and all other grades are unaffected:
>
> ```json
> { "_id" : 1, "grades" : [ 15, 92, 90 ] }
> { "_id" : 2, "grades" : [ 18, 100, 100 ] }
> { "_id" : 3, "grades" : [ 15, 100, 100 ] }
> ```

sections.heading: Delete
sections.body:
Delete operations find existing documents in a MongoDB collection and
remove them. You use standard MongoDB query syntax to specify which
documents to delete.

sections.heading: Delete a Single Document (deleteOne())
sections.body:
You can delete a single document from a collection using the
[collection.deleteOne()](collection.deleteOne) method.

The following [Atlas Function](atlas-functions) snippet deletes one document
in the `items` collection that has a `name` value of `lego`:

```javascript
const query = { "name": "lego" };

itemsCollection.deleteOne(query)
  .then(result => console.log(`Deleted ${result.deletedCount} item.`))
  .catch(err => console.error(`Delete failed with error: ${err}`))
```

Alternatively, you can update a single document using
[collection.findOneAndDelete()](collection.findOneAndDelete). This method allows you to
find, remove, and return the deleted document in a single operation.

codeBlocks.content:
const query = { "name": "lego" };

itemsCollection.deleteOne(query)
  .then(result => console.log(`Deleted ${result.deletedCount} item.`))
  .catch(err => console.error(`Delete failed with error: ${err}`))

sections.heading: Delete One or More Documents (deleteMany())
sections.body:
You can delete multiple items from a collection using the
[collection.deleteMany()](collection.deleteMany) method.

The following snippet deletes all documents in the `items` collection
that do not have any `reviews`:

```javascript
const query = { "reviews": { "$size": 0 } };

itemsCollection.deleteMany(query)
  .then(result => console.log(`Deleted ${result.deletedCount} item(s).`))
  .catch(err => console.error(`Delete failed with error: ${err}`))
```

codeBlocks.content:
const query = { "reviews": { "$size": 0 } };

itemsCollection.deleteMany(query)
  .then(result => console.log(`Deleted ${result.deletedCount} item(s).`))
  .catch(err => console.error(`Delete failed with error: ${err}`))
